messages.content:
You are a helpful coding assistant. You have access to tools. Use them when needed. Always respond in English.

IMPORTANT: All generated code MUST be written under the directory: deliverables/snake-game
Use paths relative to that directory (e.g. main.go). NEVER create new project files in the repository root, internal/, cmd/, or tests/.

PROJECT LAYOUT: minimal.
Use the smallest correct solution — often a single file (e.g. main.go) or at most 2–3 files in the workspace.
Do NOT create DDD layer directories (domain/application/infrastructure/interfaces) unless the user explicitly asks for them.

帮我写一个贪吃蛇的小游戏

path.description: Path to the file or directory to delete.

function.description: Replace a snippet of text in a file. Finds old_snippet and replaces with new_snippet.
function.name: edit_file

old_snippet.description: The exact text to find and replace.

path.description: Path to the file to edit.

function.description: List the contents of a directory.
function.name: list_dir

path.description: Directory path to list. Defaults to current directory.

function.description: Read the complete contents of a file.
function.name: read_file

path.description: Path to the file to read.

function.description: Search code for keywords or regex patterns.
function.name: search_code

path.description: Directory or file to search in. Defaults to current directory.

query.description: Keyword or regex pattern to search for.

function.description: Search for files by name pattern (glob-style or substring).
function.name: search_file

path.description: Directory to search in. Defaults to current directory.

pattern.description: Pattern to match against file names. Supports * wildcard.

function.description: Execute a shell command with a 30-second timeout. Uses cmd /C on Windows and sh -c on Linux/macOS (any CPU arch).
function.name: shell_exec

workdir.description: Working directory for the command. Defaults to current directory.

function.description: Create a new file or overwrite an existing file with the given content.
function.name: write_file

content.description: Content to write to the file.

path.description: Path to the file to write.
